previewBanner: Design preview — mirrors the production homepage. Route / is the shipped landing.

hero.title: What runs on your server?
hero.description: Appliance platform for your host. Storage, services, and administration run on the device — not in someone else's cloud.
hero.ctaDownloadHint: Install media ships when publicly released

heroStory.ariaLabel: Appliance signal path from hardware to control center

installJourney.heading: From install media to a running appliance
installJourney.intro: Four phases from first boot to a reachable admin surface. Media and step-by-step detail ship with the public release — the flow stays the same.

steps.title: Create boot media
steps.body: Write the install image to USB or equivalent and verify against the published checksum — only once the image exists.

steps.title: Install on target hardware
steps.body: EFI-capable x86_64 host as the appliance target. Partitioning and system setup run in the installer — not a container-only deployment.

steps.title: Network and reachability
steps.body: Make the appliance reachable on the local network. Initial setup and the control center run same-origin on the device.

steps.title: Sign in locally and verify
steps.body: Log in on the appliance, then use the read-only overview with real API data. More modules only when the hardware supports them.

operateJourney.heading: Day-to-day after installation
operateJourney.intro: Storage, services, and status work on one host — documented like server operations, not SaaS ops with a foreign control plane.

steps.title: Structure storage
steps.body: Pools, shares, and persistent state under a clear boundary. Changes apply on the device, not in an external admin service.

steps.title: Run host services
steps.body: Start, stop, and monitor systemd-managed processes in the host context — without multi-tenant abstraction.

steps.title: Read status from the appliance
steps.body: Overview and future modules only with real API data. Public pages do not show live values for that.

steps.title: Plan release changes
steps.body: Version changes through published media, documented steps, and release notes — not hidden auto-updates.

release.label: Release status
release.title: Public install media not available yet
release.body: Vyntrio OS is in active development. Artifact status, requirements, and workflow are visible here — no file, no version number, and no invented timeline.

pillars.heading: Storage, services, control
pillars.intro: Three layers on one device: disk, processes, administration — no separate cloud layer.

storage.title: State on disk
storage.body: Persistent appliance state under a clear operations boundary — prepared for pools, shares, and controlled administration on the target device.

services.title: Processes on the host
services.body: Systemd-managed services on the hardware itself. No multi-tenant SaaS, no foreign control plane.

control.title: Local control center
control.body: After sign-in you get a read-only overview with real API data. Additional modules appear only when the appliance supports them.

surfaceShowcase.heading: The admin surface at a glance
surfaceShowcase.intro: This is what the control center looks like — static, no live data, same surface as after sign-in.

surfacePreview.heading: Vyntrio Control Center
surfacePreview.panelNote: Instance name, readiness, and modules appear only after successful sign-in to a real appliance.

productStatus.heading: What this page does not show
productStatus.body: Public pages describe the product. They do not fake operations, availability, or admin actions.
points: No live metrics on marketing pages
Live status only in the authenticated appliance view

finalCta.heading: Next steps
finalCta.body: When install media is ready, head to the release area. If you already run an appliance, sign in on the device.

hero.title: Install media and artifact status
hero.description: Artifact status, requirements, and the planned install flow in one place. Published images appear with checksum and release notes — not before.

panel.heading: Artifact status
panel.intro: All entries are static and will be filled with the first public release. There is currently no file to download.
panel.note: On release, filename, checksum, signature status, and release notes appear here. Until then there is no hidden download link.

readiness.heading: What to clarify before your first install attempt
readiness.intro: These points help you judge whether your setup fits the appliance model — whether or not media is published yet.

items.title: Dedicated host
items.description: Target is a physical server or homelab host — not a container-only or VM-only setup as the long-term solution.

items.title: EFI boot
items.description: Firmware must support UEFI boot. Legacy BIOS-only systems are not the target model.

items.title: Console or out-of-band
items.description: First install needs local access to the device — monitor, KVM, or an equivalent path.

items.title: Local network
items.description: Initial setup and the control center run on the LAN, same-origin on the appliance.

items.title: Data on target disks
items.description: Install can overwrite existing data on the target medium. Clarify your backup plan before the first attempt.

installOutline.heading: Planned install workflow
installOutline.intro: This is the path from media to a reachable appliance once published images exist. Until then it orients you on what to expect.
installOutline.ariaLabel: Planned install workflow for Vyntrio OS

steps.title: Download and verify image
steps.body: Fetch the published install image and verify against the listed checksum — no blind download without verification.

steps.title: Write boot media
steps.body: Write the image to USB or equivalent. Boot the device from that medium, not from the existing disk.

steps.title: Run the installer
steps.body: Set EFI boot order, run the installer, and choose the target disk deliberately. Partitioning follows the release — not invented here.

steps.title: Configure network
steps.body: Make the appliance reachable on the LAN. IP, gateway, and DNS are set on the device — not through a foreign admin cloud.

steps.title: First sign-in
steps.body: Sign in locally on the device and open the control center. Overview then shows real API data for the instance.

steps.title: Follow ops docs
steps.body: Use the docs structure for storage, services, and maintenance. Release changes stay tied to published media.

mediaPrep.heading: Prepare boot media
mediaPrep.intro: Once an install image is published, writing and verifying the medium is the first step — before booting the target device.

items.title: Verify before writing
items.description: Check the image file against SHA-256 from the release area. A mismatch means: do not boot.

items.title: USB or equivalent
items.description: Bootable medium with enough capacity for the image. Exact format follows the publication.

items.title: Test EFI boot
items.description: After writing, boot from the medium once before touching production data on the target host.

requirements.heading: Hardware and environment requirements
requirements.intro: Baseline picture for a target appliance device. Concrete limits (RAM, disk size) ship with the release — no invented numbers here.

items.title: x86_64 with UEFI
items.description: 64-bit host with EFI-capable firmware. Primary target: dedicated server or homelab hardware.

items.title: Memory
items.description: RAM needs depend on storage and service load. Binding minimums appear in the release — not before.

items.title: Target disk
items.description: Persistent storage for system and appliance data. Size and layout are documented with the install image.

items.title: Network connection
items.description: Ethernet on the local network for initial setup and day-to-day use. No internet requirement for basic administration.

items.title: Physical access
items.description: Monitor, keyboard, or KVM for first install and recovery — even when much later work happens over the LAN.

inlineCta.heading: Back to product preview
inlineCta.body: Release details stay on this page. For appliance structure context, start on the preview homepage.

productionInlineCta.heading: Continue on the site
productionInlineCta.body: Release details stay on this page. For appliance structure context, start on the homepage.

hero.title: Documentation for install and operations
hero.description: Topics for getting started, installation, day-to-day use, and recovery — static structure, no live search, no hidden release files.

guide.heading: How this documentation is organized
guide.body: Sections follow the path from first install to running operations. Install media and artifact status stay in the release area — not hidden inside docs.

sections.heading: First steps
sections.intro: What to know before and right after install — without front-running step-by-step detail that ships with real media.

items.title: Read the release area
items.description: Artifact status, checksum, and companion docs start at /download — not in a hidden footer link.

items.title: Install workflow
items.description: Steps from boot medium to a reachable appliance — fully documented once the image is published.

items.title: First sign-in
items.description: Local device login and transition into the control center with real API data for the instance.

items.title: Release notes
items.description: Version-specific changes, breaking changes, and known limits — only after an actual release.

sections.heading: Installation in detail
sections.intro: Reference for media, hardware, and initial configuration — complements the workflow in the release area with operations context.

items.title: Boot media and verification
items.description: Write the image, verify checksum, test EFI boot. Do not boot faulty media on a production host.

items.title: Partitioning and target disk
items.description: Which disks are used for system and data follows the release installer — not this overview.

items.title: Network on first boot
items.description: Set IP, gateway, and DNS on the device. Confirm LAN reachability before further steps.

items.title: Post-install checks
items.description: Overview in the control center: instance name, readiness, and supported modules — only after real sign-in.

sections.heading: Appliance in daily use
sections.intro: Storage, services, and control on the host — documented like a server product, not a SaaS dashboard.

items.title: Storage & shares
items.description: Pools, shares, and persistent state under a clear operations boundary on the device.

items.title: Host services
items.description: Manage systemd-driven processes on the hardware — start, stop, dependencies.

items.title: Control center
items.description: Read-only overview and future modules only when the appliance actually provides them.

items.title: Access and sign-in
items.description: Sessions and admin actions run same-origin on the appliance — no separate cloud login.

sections.heading: Recovery and release changes
sections.intro: What applies to problems, backups, and version changes — without promising unimplemented recovery tools.

items.title: Backup before changes
items.description: Back up configuration and data before changing storage layout or release version.

items.title: Recovery media
items.description: Whether and how recovery images ship with a release is stated in release notes — not before.

items.title: Release upgrade
items.description: Version changes only through published media and documented steps — no silent background update.

items.title: Public docs boundary
items.description: Live status, metrics, and admin actions appear only after sign-in to a real appliance.

inlineCta.heading: Continue to release preview
inlineCta.body: Documentation and install media stay separate. For artifact status, switch to the release area.

productionInlineCta.heading: Continue to release area
productionInlineCta.body: Documentation and install media stay separate. For artifact status, switch to the release area.

footer.tagline: Vyntrio OS — homelab appliance for storage, services, and control.
footer.previewNote: Design preview Block 11R.9 — lazy-loaded, not in the `/` bundle
